body_txt: The common nerd is, at worst, a bit annoying. Sure, he might have No Social Skills and be overly fond of telling people about his Pokémon collection, but come on — he wouldn't hurt a fly. And then there's this jerk (usually but definitely not always a guy ). Either his lack of social niceties is caused by a complete disregard for other people, or else his loneliness and constant rejection has turned him bitter and twisted. Either way, he's out to get everything he's ever wanted, by any means necessary, including revenge for any real or imagined slights he's suffered. He's often an Entitled Bastard who thinks that his superior intelligence (which may or may not be as superior as he imagines ) should automatically make him adored and successful, and that the people failing to give him his due deserve some sort of exaggerated punishment for it. He often does suffer from genuine bullying and mistreatment, but by the time he gets to his Motive Rant , he's done so many horrible things that his audience will be less than sympathetic — no matter how many times you were stuffed in a locker or given a wedgie , it just doesn't justify sending demons to rip the living souls out of peoples' bodies. He will also often seek to get a girlfriend, but he fails to seduce a girl due to being unattractive and having a poor fashion sense and No Social Skills . So, he'll usually resort to immoral methods, such as kidnapping the girl or using Mind Control to force her to be his girlfriend. The Nerdy Bully and Insufferable Genius are this trope's somewhat more benign cousins, who settle for being Jerkasses instead of going straight for outright villainy. note These examples are usually more impressive than the Evil Nerd as well: He may think he's a genius, but whether that's true or not, he tends to be too afraid of direct confrontation to become a successful bully. On the flip side, the Evil Genius , Evil Overlord , or Supervillain are what the Evil Nerd often wants to be but can't quite live up to — regardless of his actual intelligence, he's fundamentally too petty and narrow-minded to conceive of a genuinely visionary plan, evil or otherwise. In other words, an Evil Nerd is to an Evil Genius what a Smug Snake is to a Magnificent Bastard . Since the Evil Nerd is generally not the fittest or best-looking, there is likely to be overlap with Brains Evil, Brawn Good and Beauty Equals Goodness as he faces off against the more attractive and physically capable heroes. Compare the Evil Cripple , whose broken morality is symbolised and/or caused by a more visible handicap, as well as Nerdy Bully , which he may overlap with or have graduated from if he's past his high school years. If the Evil Nerd is also a businessperson, see Tech Bro . Contrast Nerd in Evil's Helmet , where the villain's nerdiness offsets his evil instead of being the foundation of it. Contrast Abhorrent Alpha Male , for malevolent manly men. No Real Life Examples, Please! We all know that not all nerds in Real Life are nice people.
